_comment: This is your personal settings file. Everything the tools need is here. Edit it once, and the tools do the rest. Nothing is uploaded anywhere.
name: Your Name

resume_variants._comment: Reusable resume files for fast applying. Put each .docx in this folder. The apply flow suggests a variant based on the job title, but you can override per role. Leave a filename blank to fall back to resume_file.

target_roles._comment: Words that should appear in job titles you want. Keep it broad. These are OR-matched (any one is enough).

locations._comment: home_city drives the 'My city' filter. remote_scope is the region you'd take remote roles in (e.g. 'US'). Leave preferred empty [] unless you want to hard-limit the list.

location_filter._comment: Discards irrelevant roles at pull time so they never clutter your list. Keeps ONLY roles that are local (within max_miles of your area) OR remote-US. Set enabled:false to keep everything.

adzuna._comment: FREE. Broad aggregator, searches by location+radius. Key at developer.adzuna.com (5 min). Leave app_id blank to skip.

usajobs._comment: FREE. Federal jobs — real roles with real headcount (no phantom postings). Key at developer.usajobs.gov. user_agent must be the email you registered with. Leave api_key blank to skip.

jooble._comment: FREE. Large aggregator. Request a key at jooble.org/api/about. Leave api_key blank to skip.

jsearch._comment: PAID (~$15/mo). Taps Google Jobs = the broadest single source. Key at rapidapi.com/letscrape-6bRBa3QguO5/api/jsearch. Leave api_key blank to skip.

work_types._comment: Which arrangements you'll consider. Options: Remote, Hybrid, On-site.

companies._comment: Mix of national AI/SaaS (remote roles) + LA-area companies (local on-site/hybrid). Many slugs go stale — run diagnose.py to see which work and prune the 404s. Add your own: the slug is the word in the company's careers URL (boards.greenhouse.io/THIS).

resume_keywords._comment: AUTO-FILLED from your resume by setup.py. This is what job postings get scored against. You can edit it, but you usually won't need to.
